text: After finishing my work, I sat by the window with tea and felt my breathing slow down naturally. There was no urgency in me.
I took a long walk without checking my phone once, and my mind felt clear and spacious. The whole evening felt easy.
I feel settled in my body, with loose shoulders and steady breath. Nothing feels rushed.
Even with tasks remaining, I feel calm and confident that they can wait their turn.
My thoughts are slow and organized, and I can focus on one thing at a time without strain.
I am resting comfortably and noticing small details around me with quiet attention.
I feel emotionally unburdened, with a gentle mood and steady concentration.
There is no internal pressure right now; my pace feels natural and unhurried.
I can handle uncertainty without overthinking it. My nervous system feels calm and regulated.
I feel grounded and present, with smooth breathing and no mental noise.
I am in a restful state where conversation, work, and silence all feel manageable.
My body feels soft rather than tense, and my attention is steady rather than scattered.
I feel restored after a quiet morning, and my thoughts are clear and gentle.
I can move through decisions calmly without replaying every possibility.
I feel at ease, mentally open, and comfortably relaxed in this moment.
